title: ignition doesn't follow symlinks in ~/.config/containers/certs.d/
body:
### Issue Description

I have multiple registries all using a certificate signed by the same ca, so I use the following directory structure in `~/.config/containers/certs.d/`:

```
~/.config/containers/certs.d > ls -alh
total 0
drwxr-xr-x  6 ich  staff   192B  6 Feb 15:52 .
drwxr-xr-x@ 8 ich  staff   256B  6 Feb 15:42 ..
drwxr-xr-x  3 ich  staff    96B  4 Feb 14:13 nexus.example.com:5000
lrwxr-xr-x  1 ich  staff    25B  6 Feb 15:52 nexus.example.com:5001 -> nexus.example.com:5000
lrwxr-xr-x  1 ich  staff    25B  6 Feb 15:52 nexus.example.com:5002 -> nexus.example.com:5000
lrwxr-xr-x  1 ich  staff    25B  6 Feb 15:52 nexus.example.com:5003 -> nexus.example.com:5000

~/.config/containers/certs.d > find .
.
./nexus.example.com:5001
./nexus.example.com:5000
./nexus.example.com:5000/ca.crt
./nexus.example.com:5002
./nexus.example.com:5003
```

After ignition, the podman vm has only `/etc/containers/certs.d/nexus.example.com:5000/ca.crt` leading to certificate issues when pulling images from the other registries.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create a folder structure as described above
2. start a new podman vm
3. pull or push an image to a registry, whose `ca.crt` is in a symlinked directory.


### Describe the results you received

`/etc/containers/certs.d/` within the podman vm is missing the folders `nexus.example.com:5001`, nexus.example.com:5002` and `nexus.example.com:5003`, so I got certificate issues when pulling or pushing images to these registries.

### Describe the results you expected

`/etc/containers/certs.d/` within the podman vm should match `~/.config/containers/certs.d/`, but I don't care if the symlink are preserved or not.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I guess the issue is that `filepath.WalkDir` doesn't follow symlinks.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/757c6215833ad5b4b1c72f4e26fbe46eecfa3c89/pkg/machine/ignition/ignition.go#L456-L471

comments.body: It would be nice, if there is an ignition to put a certificate in `/etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors`, so using `certs.d` isn't needed.

ignition is not a good mechanism for this because on some providers, the time it takes to to be injected is unacceptable.  we have recently added a `--playbook` to `podman machine init`  that can do this nicely.  We also have a `podman machine cp` command being worked on rn where you can take something from the host and put it into the machine.

I've just migrated from Docker Desktop to Podman Desktop, so this is all new to me.

Using an Ansible playbook sounds right for the provision of certs under `/etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors`. But I don't see a way to configure and edit the playbook within Podman Desktop, so I'm hesitated to propagate this approach to my team. And according to the docs, the playbook is only executed after the first boot. That's a pity, as it doesn't allow to reconfigure the vm afterwards. 

But fair enough, that's an issue with the ignition of `/etc/containers/certs.d/certs.d` as well: Having to remove and recreate the VM just to add a certificate doesn't feel right.

I think this is simply because we use filepath.WalDir() which does not follow symlinks here
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2cbb5fead9712b9b1634171f4106db1b47ccf026/pkg/machine/ignition/ignition.go#L456-L476

I think adding an extra case there to preserve the symlinks should be easy enough to add.
